recurringPattern: Carousel or grid of testimonial cards with a circular headshot, quote in italic or large quotation marks, person's name/title below, and star ratings. Cards use white background on a tinted section background. Carousel variant includes dot indicators and left/right arrows.
designHarm: The testimonial carousel reduces trust signals to decorative filler — users have learned to ignore auto-rotating quotes. The identical card format implies testimonials are stock imagery, not genuine endorsements. Star ratings appear even for B2B enterprise products where star reviews are not the norm.
legitimateExceptions: Consumer product pages with verified user reviews that need aggregate display
Case study sections where long-form quotes need compact card previews
possibleFixtures: Standard testimonial carousel with headshots and stars
Full case study layout with video and metrics instead of quote cards
Simple quote list without avatars or ratings
originalObservations: AI consistently generates circular headshots with identical sizing even when the number of testimonials varies widely.
Quotation marks are always large, decorative, and in an accent color.
The carousel auto-plays in generated prototypes despite known usability issues with auto-rotating testimonials.
